title: Oldenlandia decumbens
taxonomy: Oldenlandia decumbens is classified within the family Rubiaceae and the order Gentianales. The species Oldenlandia decumbens belongs to the genus Oldenlandia, which is a group of flowering plants. Oldenlandia decumbens is a type of herbaceous plant, and its classification reflects its botanical characteristics. The species is native to tropical regions and is often found in wet environments. Its classification within the Rubiaceae family is consistent with its morphological features and growth habits.

intro: Oldenlandia decumbens, also known as creeping oldenlandia or creeping lophostemon, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help in treating various ailments such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Oldenlandia decumbens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Oldenlandia decumbens is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin problems.
